title: pityopsis nervosa var. tracyi
intro: Pityopsis nervosa var. tracyi, also known as Tracy's silphium, is a plant species native to the United States, specifically in the southern and central regions. It is a perennial herb that belongs to the Asteraceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits for digestive issues, skin conditions, and wound healing. Pityopsis nervosa var. tracyi can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
